reasoning: The question asks if a hurricane stronger than Erin (160 mph) will occur in the 2025 season. Since the knowledge cutoff is October 26, 2025, and the prompt implies no such storm has formed yet (as it references Milton in 2024 as the most recent extremely powerful storm), we must estimate the probability of a >160 mph storm forming between Oct 26 and Dec 1. Historically, late-season hurricanes exceeding 160 mph are extremely rare (only the 1932 Cuba hurricane has done so historically). The base rate is thus well under 1%. Allowing for climate-driven temperature increases and the possibility of a currently active but unmentioned storm, 0.05 is a safely bounded and well-calibrated estimate for this extremely unlikely late-season event.
